methods.description: Procedure for analyzing protein expression in tobacco leaves using immunoblotting techniques.

steps.description: Tobacco leaves (~ 10 mg) sampled 3 days after infiltration were ground in 150 µl sample buffer (Wako Pure Chemical Industries, Osaka, Japan) containing 5% β-mercaptoethanol.
steps.searchable_summary: Grind tobacco leaves in sample buffer with β-mercaptoethanol

steps.description: Samples were denatured at 98 °C for 2 min.
Proteins were separated by 10% polyacrylamide gel (SuperSep™Ace, Wako Pure Chemical Industries) electrophoresis.
steps.searchable_summary: Separate proteins using 10% polyacrylamide gel electrophoresis

steps.description: Proteins were transferred to PVDF membranes (Immobilon, Millipore) by semidry electroblotting.
steps.searchable_summary: Transfer proteins to PVDF membranes using semidry electroblotting

steps.description: Blots were probed with anti-GFP primary antibody (SAB4301138, Sigma-Aldrich Co. LLC) and alkaline phosphatase conjugated anti-mouse secondary antibody (A3562, Sigma-Aldrich Co. LLC).
steps.searchable_summary: Probe blots with primary and secondary antibodies
inputs: Alkaline phosphatase conjugated anti-mouse secondary antibody

steps.description: Visualized using WesternBlue ® Stabilized substrate for alkaline phosphatase (Promega).
